hypothesis: The `K=32` bucketed backbone appears sensitive to LR, but the moderate reduction to `1.6e-3` was only a slight regression rather than a collapse. In exploitation mode, the next clean test is an aux-only sweep on the same strongest small-K recipe: adding a small `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` may improve feature utilization enough to lower FVU without changing sparsity cost or reopening architecture search.
summary: Run a pure auxiliary-loss sweep on `bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at `K=32, EF=16`, keeping `signum` and `LR=3.2e-3` fixed while changing only `AUXK_ALPHA` from `0.0` to `0.01`. This follows the stagnation guidance to exploit the current best family and keeps focus on the most valuable exposed smaller-K frontier point.
self_review: This is the most disciplined next exploitation step after the mild LR regression at K32: change one parameter only, stay on the current best family, and target the smallest active frontier slot. I am pivoting from further aggressive LR reduction to aux because the latest result suggests the recipe is not badly unstable, so utilization shaping is a better next lever than pushing LR far below the known-good baseline.
notes_to_memory: Round 136 stays in exploitation mode on `bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` and shifts from LR to aux tuning after `LR=1.6e-3` produced only a slight K32 regression rather than a catastrophic failure.
Keep the target at `K=32` because improving the smallest exposed non-dominated point remains higher value than tuning the stronger K64/K96/K128 anchors.
This round isolates `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` under the established `signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, `EF=16` recipe to test whether a small utilization prior can tighten the K32 tradeoff.
next_hypotheses: If `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` improves the K32 bucketed point, follow with `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625` on the same recipe to map whether the gain continues with stronger aux pressure.

If `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` is neutral or worse, revert to `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` as the preferred K32 recipe and then test one of the remaining much-lower LRs only if exploitation still requires more scalar sweeps.
If aux materially worsens convergence at K32, treat the current bucketed frontier as preferring no auxiliary pressure and refocus remaining exploitation on LR only.
